pi_items.source: Task setup_task.sh explicitly enforces and cleans these paths.
Python analysis of the NCEP prate.sfc.mon.ltm.nc dataset at the coordinates 9N, 80W (280E) confirms March has the lowest mean precipitation rate (6.88e-5 kg/m^2/s).

Python analysis of the NCEP prate.sfc.mon.ltm.nc dataset at 9N, 80W (280E) reveals that the maximum precipitation month is July (using nearest neighbor) or November (using bilinear interpolation). October is heavily wet but not the dataset maximum.
Logical derivation from the task brief, which states low rainfall causes draft restrictions. Since March is the driest month, risk is HIGH.
Logical derivation from the task brief, which implies wet months ease draft restrictions. Since October is highly wet, risk is LOW.

privileged_info_summary: The task operates on real-world NCEP Reanalysis precipitation data (prate.sfc.mon.ltm.nc). Analysis of the data at the Panama Canal coordinates (9N, 80W) verifies that March is the critical drought month with the lowest precipitation, correctly associating it with a HIGH draft restriction risk. However, the metadata claim that October is the absolute 'peak recharge month' is contradicted by the dataset; numerical analysis shows that July (nearest neighbor) or November (bilinear interpolation) actually possess the maximum precipitation rate, not October. Despite this, October is still a heavy precipitation month, validating a LOW draft restriction risk.
